# feat(runtime): wire arm_time_limits into Parallel/ParallelCyclic/Docker (#912 Shard 6)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `e254d22bbf39` — feat(runtime): wire arm_time_limits into Parallel/ParallelCyclic/Docker (#912 Shard 6)

**Body**:

Same fake-dispatch failure mode as LocalRuntime — these three runtimes
accepted soft_time_limit/time_limit kwargs but only validated and
dropped them. Wire enforcement so the typed exceptions actually raise.

ParallelRuntime (async):
  - arm_time_limits_async around the parallel-DAG execution.
  - Post-completion poll for hard_deadline_reached + soft-fired-but-
    completed paths.
  - Typed deadline exception re-raise above the broad RuntimeExecutionError
    wrapper so the typed exception propagates untouched.
  - WorkflowCancelledError classifier for inter-node cancel observation.

ParallelCyclicRuntime (sync):
  - arm_time_limits around all three execution paths (cyclic /
    parallel-DAG / LocalRuntime fallback). Forwards typed kwargs to
    LocalRuntime so the inner wiring also fires (defense-in-depth).
  - Same post-completion poll + typed exception passthrough.

DockerRuntime (out-of-process):
  - arm_time_limits around the per-node container-execution loop.
  - Per-node-boundary poll: between containers, check if the hard
    timer fired during the previous container and raise.
  - Inherent constraint of out-of-process execution: long-running
    single-node Docker workflows cannot be interrupted mid-container
    (the timer cannot inject into a running `docker run` subprocess).
    Multi-node Docker workflows DO get the full deadline contract
    at every node boundary.

AccessControlledRuntime (#6) and DurableExecutionEngine (#7) require
NO changes — both already forward typed kwargs by name to their inner
runtime's execute call, so the inner wiring (LocalRuntime /
AsyncLocalRuntime) fires the deadline contract automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
